Florida · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist Guide

Issued by the Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Council under the Florida Department of Health, Board of Medicine. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$165
$80 application + $80 license + $5 unlicensed activity fee
Renewal
$95
Every 2 years + 30 CE hours
Processing Time
30 days (initial review)
Subject to board volume
License Duration
Up to 2 Years (expires May 31 odd years)
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Council

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 6330
Tallahassee, FL 32314-6330

Physical Address

4052 Bald Cypress Way, Bin C-05
Tallahassee, FL 32399-3255

Phone & Fax

Fax: (850) 414-6860

Email

mqa.dieteticsnutrition@flhealth.gov

Credential Categories

Florida Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist

Florida issues a combined credential. Requires minimum bachelor's degree, 900 hours of supervised practice, and passing a licensing examination (typically the CDR).

Licensed Nutrition Counselor

An alternative credential path in Florida; requirements vary.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Florida Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist

Registration and Education

Complete your degree and supervised practice. Most applicants use their active CDR registration as proof of passing a recognized examination.

Apply Online

Submit your application for Licensure by Endorsement or Examination through the Florida Department of Health MQA Services portal and pay the $165 total fee.

Background Screening

Undergo fingerprinting for a criminal history background check through a Livescan service provider.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official transcriptsMust be sent directly from the educational institution.
  • Proof of Examination or CDRVerification sent directly to the Board.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Florida offers a temporary permit for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the examination. It requires an additional fee and direct supervision.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Florida allows Licensure by Endorsement for professionals who hold a current, active license in another state with equivalent or more stringent requirements.

Frequently Asked Questions

Florida Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Florida dietitian license cost?

The Florida Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Council charges a total initial fee of $165. This includes a $50 application fee, $110 license fee, and $5 unlicensed activity fee.

Is a background check required for a Florida dietitian license?

Yes. All applicants must undergo a Livescan fingerprint background check as part of the application process with the Florida Department of Health.
